category_title: Students with severe hearing impairment and EAL who use physical gesture to demonstrate understanding
minimal_context: The student is working in a collaborative group to read aloud scripts from A Midsummer Night's Dream.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please get into your collaborative groups and start reading your scripts for A Midsummer Night's Dream.

STUDENT: sometime um there are people that they are don’t take the [cope]

TUTOR: Let's just focus on the script for now. It is your turn to read the next part.

STUDENT: (Stays very quiet, points to the script, and writes down exactly one sentence on his paper instead of speaking.)

TUTOR: You need to read the lines aloud to the class, not just write them down. Go ahead and speak.

STUDENT: (Attempts to read the Shakespearean text aloud to the class but has great difficulty with pronouncing words, resulting in very basic verbal communication that is hard to understand.)

options.A: Establish an inclusive ecology for all students by designing lessons to benefit the class collectively, rather than singling out and differentiating for individuals.
options.B: Transition the decision as to when, and for how long, to take quiet time from the tutor to the student during the lessons.
options.C: Design the script materials with detailed pictures of each scene for the project-based lessons to use during the collaborative group work.
options.D: Require that each script reading undergoes three practice revisions during the collaborative group work, and only the final performance is evaluated.
